title: Protein Mocha
tagline: Post-workout chocolate mocha — chocolate protein powder blended into coffee and warm milk without clumping.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (1 scoop whey + 1 cup milk + coffee).
Already a high-protein drink (~32 g) for ~250 kcal — a clean post-workout refuel with a caffeine hit for recovery.
To cut calories, use toned/skim milk or a milk alternative; to push protein higher, use 1.5 scoops and a splash more liquid.
No soya boost needed — the whey is the protein here.

items: Optional: honey, maple syrup or a pinch of cinnamon

steps.title: Brew the coffee
steps.body: Prepare 1 cup of coffee however you like it.

steps.title: Warm the milk
steps.body: Heat 1 cup milk until warm but not boiling. Keep it below ~55–65°C — if the liquid is too hot the protein powder clumps and can denature into an unpleasant texture.

steps.title: Make a smooth paste
steps.body: In a shaker or cup, mix 1 scoop chocolate protein powder with a small amount of the warm milk (or a splash of room-temperature liquid) first, stirring into a smooth paste. This is the trick that prevents clumps.

steps.title: Combine
steps.body: Stir or blend the protein paste into the coffee and the remaining warm milk until fully smooth.

steps.title: Sweeten & serve
steps.body: Taste and, if you like, add a little honey, maple syrup or a pinch of cinnamon. Drink post-workout while warm.

notes: The whole game here is temperature and mixing order: paste first with cooler liquid, then add the warm coffee/milk — that's what keeps it silky instead of grainy.
Works as a warm post-workout drink; pour over ice for an iced version.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
